title: Bridge Essay: Head-direction cells as a neural compass in bats navigating outdoors on a remote

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Visionary Cosmologies** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Visionary cosmologies — self-transcendence, mystical experience, numinous, awe, cosmic consciousness.

**Head-direction cells as aneural compass in bats navigating outdoors on aremote oceanic island.** — Science. PMID: 41100626. DOI: 10.1126/science.adw6202. Animals and humans rely on their navigation skills to survive. However, spatial neurons in the brain's "navigation circuit" had not previously been studied under real-world conditions. We conducted an electrophysiological study of spatial neurons in the wild: We recorded head-direction cells from the presubiculum of bats flying unconstrained and navigating outdoors on aremote oceanic island.
